name: tsql-step-debugger
description: Client-side, interpreter-driven step debugger for T-SQL stored procedures, functions, and scripts (no engine-side debug support required).

tsqlDbg.adapterPath.description: Dev override: absolute path to a locally-built TsqlDbg.Adapter executable, instead of the one bundled with this extension.

server.description: Server name (host, host\instance, or host,port). Leave server/database out to pick a saved connection from the Connection Manager at launch.

authType.description: 'integrated' = Windows/SSPI (default); 'sql' = SQL login (set 'sqlUser'; the password is entered in the Connection Manager and stored in VS Code SecretStorage — never in launch.json).

sqlUser.description: SQL login name when authType is 'sql'. The password is NEVER a launch-config field — it is stored in SecretStorage and passed to the adapter via a child-process environment variable.

mode.description: Debug a deployed module, or the active .sql file as frame 0.

procedure.description: Two/three-part procedure name. Required when mode = procedure.

script.description: Script path. Required when mode = script.

targetsFile.description: Path to targets.json. Defaults to MSSQL_DEBUG_TARGETS env var, then ${workspaceFolder}/targets.json.

commitMode.description: On an explicit Stop/terminate you'll be asked to confirm in a modal naming the server/database — that confirmation is the authorization (no targets.json allowWrites pre-gate on the interactive VS Code surface). Declining, a 60s timeout, or any other end (disconnect, error) always rolls back instead.

stopOnEntry.description: Publish a stop at frame 0's first statement before running anything, instead of running to completion.

waitfor.description: 'skip': WAITFOR DELAY/TIME is intercepted and logged, not sent to the server. 'honor': sent as-is (the session genuinely blocks for the delay).

compatLevel.description: T-SQL parser (ScriptDom) version. 0 = auto: detect the server's version on connect and pick the matching parser (clamped to 150-170, with a warning if the server is older/newer). Or pin one: 150 (SQL 2019), 160 (SQL 2022), 170 (SQL 2025).

boost.description: Execute whole IF/WHILE subtrees as one composed batch under continue, instead of interpreting statement by statement. Off by default.

executeAs.description: Clause text emitted after 'EXECUTE AS' at session init (e.g. "USER = 'AppUser'"), REVERTed in teardown. Omit for no impersonation.

allowConsoleWrites.description: Allow the Debug Console to run DML/DDL and variable writes (not just SELECT), still inside the session transaction (rolled back at end unless commitMode:commit). Set false for a read-only console.

consoleTimeoutSec.description: Command timeout for Debug Console (REPL) evaluations, distinct from commandTimeoutSec.

maxConsoleRows.description: Row cap for Debug Console result-set rendering ("200 of N — refine your query").

tempTablePageSize.description: Rows per page when browsing the Temp Tables scope.

displayValueChars.description: Max characters shown per variable/cell value before truncation.

watchBudgetMs.description: Shared per-stop time budget for evaluating watch expressions before remaining ones show a click-to-evaluate placeholder.

sourceMap.description: Glob patterns (e.g. "${workspaceFolder}/procs/**/*.sql") checked against each module's server definition (CRLF-normalized byte match); a match binds breakpoints and stack frames to that real file instead of the read-only tsqldbg: document.

logLevel.description: Debug Console verbosity. 'normal' (default) shows your script's output (PRINT, SELECT result sets), errors, logpoints, and commit/rollback notices. 'verbose' additionally shows the debugger's own diagnostic annotations: the NOCOUNT-forced-ON note, '-- GO: entering batch k of N', the untracked-SET-options note, and the DML-trigger heads-up. Distinct from 'trace' (the adapter-internal log file).

configurationSnippets.label: T-SQL: Debug Script
configurationSnippets.description: Debug the active .sql file — pick a connection at launch (Connection Manager).
